name: orchestrator-framework-error-boundary
description: FrameworkError adoption is scoped to the supervisor.run() boundary only: run_flow_once/run_batch_once catch FrameworkError before the generic Exception fallback and log error_type / is_fatal / is_retryable plus the full cause chain via error.explain() as structured JSONL fields. Worker tools deliberately do NOT raise ToolError — they return {'error': ...} so the LLM can see the failure and adapt within the run.
invariants: Any future FrameworkError context= must stay query-shape-only (no row contents / PII), per the mysql logging rule
Tools must keep catching their own exceptions and returning {'error': ...} strings rather than raising, or a single tool failure would crash the whole supervisor loop
contracts: _framework_error_fields(e) yields {error_type, is_fatal, is_retryable, error_chain} for JSONL grep
